01_initial_request.target_name: clear cell renal cancer (ccRCC) tumor proteome
01_initial_request.input_data: 165 FFPE tumor samples from patients diagnosed with ccRCC
01_initial_request.user_intent: To explore the tumor proteome of localized ccRCC and identify altered pathways for new therapeutic targets
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Classification of tumors into proteomics subtypes and identification of differentially expressed proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Research requires tumor samples from ccRCC patients for proteomics analysis. [Gap] No samples collected yet for this study. [Decision] To achieve proteome characterization, chose to use biobank resources to perform sample collection on 165 FFPE tumor samples from ccRCC patients, expecting to obtain patient cohort for analysis.

parameters.source: Hospital La Paz Biobank, Basque Biobank and Biobank of Servicio de Salud Andaluz

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 165 FFPE tumor samples from ccRCC patients recruited

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FFPE samples require deparaffinization before protein extraction. [Gap] Paraffin must be removed to access proteins. [Decision] To achieve protein isolation, chose to use xylene and ethanol to perform deparaffinization on FFPE sections, expecting to obtain deparaffinized tissue sections.

[Background] Deparaffinized tissue needs protein extraction and quantification. [Gap] Proteins need to be isolated and measured. [Decision] To achieve protein preparation, chose to use MicroBCA Protein Assay Kit to perform protein isolation and quantification on tissue samples, expecting to obtain protein extracts with measured quantities.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein isolates prepared and quantity measured

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein extracts need to be digested for mass spectrometry analysis. [Gap] Proteins must be converted to peptides. [Decision] To achieve MS-compatible samples, chose to use trypsin to perform protein digestion on 10 μg protein extracts, expecting to obtain peptide mixtures.

[Background] SDS from extraction buffer interferes with mass spectrometry. [Gap] Detergent must be removed before MS analysis. [Decision] To achieve clean samples, chose to use Detergent Removal Spin Columns to perform SDS elimination on lysates, expecting to obtain detergent-free peptide samples.

[Background] Peptide samples need desalting before mass spectrometry. [Gap] Salts and contaminants must be removed. [Decision] To achieve MS-ready samples, chose to use ZipTips to perform desalting on peptide samples, expecting to obtain purified peptides.

[Background] Desalted peptides need to be resolubilized for MS injection. [Gap] Peptides must be in appropriate buffer. [Decision] To achieve proper sample preparation, chose to use formic acid and acetonitrile solution to perform peptide resolubilization on dried samples, expecting to obtain peptides in MS-compatible buffer.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Samples dried and resolubilized in 0.1% formic acid and 3% acetonitrile solution

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] DDA runs are needed to build spectral library for DIA analysis. [Gap] No spectral library exists for this cohort. [Decision] To achieve spectral library generation, chose to use Orbitrap Fusion to perform data-dependent acquisition on ten pooled samples, expecting to obtain DDA spectra for library building.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DDA runs acquired for spectral library generation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Individual samples need DIA acquisition for quantification. [Gap] Proteome data not yet acquired for all 165 samples. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive proteome profiling, chose to use Orbitrap Fusion to perform data-independent acquisition on all samples, expecting to obtain DIA spectra for protein quantification.

parameters.trap_column: MZ Symmetry C18 Trap Column 5 μm, 180 μm×20mm, Waters
parameters.analytical_column: nanoEase MZ C18 HSS T3 Column 1.8 μm, 75 μm×250mm, Waters

02_agent_trajectory.observation: DIA runs acquired for all 165 ccRCC samples

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw MS data needs to be processed for protein identification and quantification. [Gap] Spectra not yet searched against database. [Decision] To achieve protein identification, chose to use Spectronaut 14 with Pulsar search engine to perform spectral searching on DDA and DIA runs, expecting to obtain identified and quantified proteins.

parameters.database: SwissProt database for human (NCBI taxonomy ID9606, release date 9 July 2019)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Identified proteins need quality filtering and missing value imputation. [Gap] Data quality not yet assessed, missing values present. [Decision] To achieve high-confidence dataset, chose to use Perseus software to perform quality filtering and missing value imputation on quantitative data, expecting to obtain filtered protein matrix.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 2026 high-confidence proteins retained after quality filtering

references.relevance: Perseus computational platform for proteomics data analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteomics subtypes need to be defined from the protein expression data. [Gap] No subtype classification exists yet. [Decision] To achieve subtype discovery, chose to use ConsensusClusterPlus package to perform consensus clustering on 2026 high-confidence proteins, expecting to obtain proteomics subtypes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two proteomics subtypes identified: CC1 (55 patients, 33%) and CC2 (110 patients, 67%)

references.relevance: Consensus clustering method for class discovery

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Differentially expressed proteins between subtypes need to be identified. [Gap] Molecular differences between CC1 and CC2 not yet characterized. [Decision] To achieve differential expression analysis, chose to use TM4 Mev software to perform SAM analysis on subtype protein profiles, expecting to identify differentially expressed proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 514 proteins showing differential expression between CC1 and CC2 subtypes identified

references.relevance: TM4 software for microarray data analysis
SAM method for identifying differential proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional relationships between proteins need to be modeled. [Gap] Network structure of proteomics data not yet established. [Decision] To achieve systems biology analysis, chose to use grapHD package to perform probabilistic graphical models on proteomics data, expecting to obtain functional network.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Network divided into eight functional nodes: complement activation, focal adhesion, splicing, translation, nucleosome, mitochondria, glycolysis, and adhesion

references.relevance: grapHD R package for high-dimensional graphical model search

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional nodes need biological interpretation. [Gap] Biological processes represented by network nodes not yet characterized. [Decision] To achieve functional annotation, chose to use DAVID webtool to perform functional enrichment analysis on network nodes, expecting to obtain biological process annotations.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Functional structure established for eight nodes with overrepresented functions characterized

references.relevance: DAVID bioinformatics resources for gene list analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Clinical relevance of subtypes needs to be assessed through survival analysis. [Gap] Survival differences between CC1 and CC2 not yet evaluated. [Decision] To achieve clinical validation, chose to use GraphPad Prism to perform Kaplan-Meier and log-rank tests on subtype survival data, expecting to obtain survival curves and statistical significance.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: No significant difference in DFS or OS between CC1 and CC2 subtypes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional node activities need to be compared between subtypes. [Gap] Biological process differences between CC1 and CC2 not yet quantified. [Decision] To achieve functional comparison, chose to use GraphPad Prism to perform Mann-Whitney tests on functional node activities between subtypes, expecting to identify significantly different pathways.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Significant differences in all nodes except complement activation; CC1 showed higher activity in glycolysis, mitochondria, translation, nucleosome, spliceosome, and adhesion2; CC2 showed higher activity in focal adhesion

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Consensus clustering with functional characterization and survival analysis

proteins_identified.interpretation: Total proteins identified from 165 ccRCC samples

high_confidence_proteins.interpretation: Proteins passing 75% valid value quality criterion

differential_proteins.interpretation: Proteins with differential expression between subtypes at FDR < 5%

survival_difference.interpretation: No significant DFS or OS difference between subtypes

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: Successfully classified ccRCC tumors into two proteomics subtypes (CC1 and CC2) with distinct molecular pathway alterations. CC1 characterized by metabolic pathways (glycolysis, mitochondria, translation), CC2 by focal adhesion and extracellular matrix pathways. 514 differentially expressed proteins identified. However, no survival difference detected between subtypes in this localized ccRCC cohort.
